commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a dedicated Control UI tool sidebar content kind, routes non-canvas tool-card clicks to that sidebar, renders expanded tool input/output, and updates related CSS, tests, and CHANGELOG.

PR surface: Source +197, Tests +179, Docs +1. Total +377 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main is source-reproducible: tool sidebar content is built as markdown, and the shared markdown renderer collapses multi-line JSON fences into a JSON disclosure.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for real Control UI proof, a dirty-branch refresh, and the unbounded parsing fix before merge review can finish.

target.title: fix(ui): show full tool input/output in tool-call sidebar
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Clicking the panel-open button on a tool card opened the side panel showing only the tool name; the actual input arguments and output payload were either missing or hidden behind a "Raw details" toggle.
- Why it matters: The side panel is the primary surface for inspecting a tool call. Users could not see what arguments a tool was invoked with or what it returned without manually expanding the inline card, which defeats the purpose of the panel button.
- What changed: Routed tool details through a dedicated `tool` `SidebarContent` kind instead of reusing the markdown renderer (which was collapsing fenced JSON into a "JSON · N lines" toggle). The sidebar now renders Tool input and Tool output as fully expanded code blocks with explicit empty states, pretty-prints valid JSON output, and exposes a "View Raw Text" fallback. Touches `ui/src/ui/sidebar-content.ts`, `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts`, `ui/src/ui/views/markdown-sidebar.ts`, `ui/src/ui/chat/chat-sidebar-raw.ts`, and `ui/src/styles/chat/sidebar.css`. Tests added in `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.node.test.ts` and `ui/src/ui/markdown.test.ts`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to tool execution, tool-card extraction in chat (inline card rendering and behavior preserved), canvas preview routing, sidebar canvas/markdown kinds, server protocol, or any non-UI surface. No changes outside `ui/`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes # <TODO: issue number, or remove this line>
- Related # <TODO: or remove>
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `renderToolCardSidebar`/the panel-open click handler built a `markdown` `SidebarContent` whose body was a fenced JSON block. The shared markdown sidebar renderer collapses fenced code blocks into a "JSON · N lines" disclosure for readability, which hid the tool input/output the user wanted to see.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no test asserting that sidebar content for a tool card surfaces both the tool input and the tool output as visible (non-collapsed) text.
- Contributing context (if known): The collapse-fenced-json heuristic in the markdown sidebar was added for assistant message readability; reusing the markdown kind for tool details meant tool payloads inherited that heuristic.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.node.test.ts` and `ui/src/ui/markdown.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: Given a tool card with both `inputText` and `outputText`, `buildToolCardSidebarContent` returns `kind: "tool"` with both `inputText` and `outputText` populated (and `outputIsJson` set when applicable, with output pretty-printed), and the markdown-sidebar render path no longer collapses tool output into a fenced-JSON toggle.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug is purely in how tool data is serialized into `SidebarContent` and how the sidebar view chooses a renderer; both can be exercised with a fast Node-level unit test against the helper plus the rendered DOM, without needing a full chat e2e.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None; prior tests only covered the markdown-collapse heuristic in isolation.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A — new tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Clicking the side-panel button on a tool card now opens a panel titled with the tool label, showing Tool input and Tool output as expanded code blocks. JSON output is pretty-printed when valid. Empty input or empty output renders an explicit empty state instead of nothing. A "View Raw Text" fallback is available.
- No config or default changes.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[click panel button on tool card]
  -> SidebarContent { kind: "markdown", content: "```json\n{...}\n```" }
  -> markdown sidebar collapses fenced JSON
  -> panel shows only tool label + "JSON · N lines" toggle

After:
[click panel button on tool card]
  -> SidebarContent { kind: "tool", toolLabel, inputText, outputText, ... }
  -> dedicated tool sidebar view
  -> panel shows tool label + expanded "Tool input" + expanded "Tool output"
     (pretty-printed JSON when valid; empty state when missing; raw-text fallback)
target.author: rrajp
